questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination indicate?
explanationSource: This sign means "Danger zone" and the additional sign stands for "Winter sports allowed". The combination indicates that winter sportspersons may use the road and road users must pay particular attention to it.
answerASource: - may be used by winter sports enthusiasts
answerBSource: - may not be used by traffic in winter
answerCSource: - may only be used with snow chains

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of in this traffic situation?
explanationSource: You must not drive over the lane boundary (solid line) to the left, which is located at the middle of the lane. Driving on it is also prohibited.
answerASource: - not cross the lane marking
answerBSource: - cross the lane marking in order to overtake
answerCSource: - cross the lane marking if oncoming traffic permits this

questionTextSource: What must you be aware of on this priority road?
explanationSource: The solid line in the middle between the two lanes prohibits crossing or crossing this line. It also prohibits parking on priority roads outside of built-up areas.
answerASource: Parking is prohibited
answerBSource: You may not cross the lane marking
answerCSource: Parking is permitted if there is a clear space of at least 3 m between a parked car and the lane marking

questionTextSource: You want to overtake. What is the right thing to do?
explanationSource: You may overtake if other road users are not endangered. The one-sided lane boundary allows you to cross the two lines from the side where the broken line is located.
answerASource: - overtake
answerBSource: - not overtake
answerCSource: - only overtake, if I remain in the right-hand lane while doing so

questionTextSource: As a resident, you want to drive into a cycle highway that has this sign. What must you be aware of when doing so?
explanationSource: A maximum speed of 30 km/h applies to traffic. Cycling traffic must not be endangered or obstructed. If necessary, motor vehicle traffic must reduce speed further. Side-by-side cycling is permitted.
answerASource: Vehicles may not be driven faster than 30 km/h
answerBSource: Cycles may travel alongside one another
answerCSource: Cycle traffic may not be impeded

questionTextSource: Who may enter a street that has this sign?
explanationSource: Vehicular traffic other than cyclists may not use cycle lanes unless permitted by additional sign. The additional sign allows residents to enter the bicycle lane.
answerASource: All cyclists
answerBSource: All persons visiting residents
answerCSource: All moped riders

questionTextSource: This road traffic sign is displayed for your lane. What must you be aware of?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates the prescribed minimum speed. You must therefore drive at least 60 km/h, provided that the road and traffic conditions allow it.
answerASource: I must drive at a minimum speed of 60 km/h, if permitted by the road and traffic conditions
answerBSource: I may only use this lane if my vehicle can and is allowed to drive at a speed of least 60 km/h
answerCSource: I may not drive faster than 60 km/h

questionTextSource: What situation applies according to this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The additional sign limits the speed limit between 10 pm and 6 am.
answerASource: - applies between 10 pm and 6 am
answerBSource: - applies between 6 am and 10 pm
answerCSource: - does not apply to electric vehicles

questionTextSource: What should you do when you see this road sign?
explanationSource:
The arrow on this traffic sign always shows the direction of travel. If it is a straight arrow, you have to turn in front of the sign. If it is a curved arrow, you must turn according to the sign. 
You have to flash to indicate the change in driving direction.
answerASource: - indicate left
answerBSource: - turn left before the traffic sign
answerCSource: - turn left behind the traffic sign

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign grants you priority in a specific place. Thus, the right of way for the driver is limited and has no general validity.
answerASource: - only at the next crossroads or junction
answerBSource: - at all crossroads and junctions on this road
answerCSource: - as far as the urban limit

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates that the motorway starts here. This also gives effect to all the rules that apply to this particular type of road.
answerASource: The start of an autobahn
answerBSource: The end of an autobahn
answerCSource: The start of a clearway for motor vehicles only

questionTextSource: What do the white markings on the road mean in this situation?
explanationSource: At intersections and junctions with “right before left” regulation, especially in confusing places, are also used by waiting lines. Wartelinia are often used that look as similar to many small way to go-along.
answerASource: - highlight a duty to give-way due to the right-before-left rule
answerBSource: - indicate a traffic-calming zone
answerCSource: - are a warning about the poor state of the road surface

questionTextSource: What is the rule created by the road markings in this situation?
explanationSource:
You have to nourish yourself this intersection carefully, as it applies right to the left.
At intersections and junctions with “right before left” regulation, especially in confusing places, are also used by waiting lines. Wartelinia are often used that look as similar to many small way to go-along.
answerASource: - approach the junction cautiously
answerBSource: - give way to the [red vehicle]
answerCSource: - come to a stop in front of the road markings

questionTextSource: Which traffic sign gives right of way at the next crossroads?
explanationSource: Only the first traffic sign gives you right of way at the next intersection. The second traffic sign, however, indicates the end of the priority road and traffic sign number three regulates the right of way at a bottleneck.
answerASource: Traffic sign 1
answerBSource: Traffic sign 2
answerCSource: Traffic sign 3

questionTextSource: Which traffic sign gives right of way?
explanationSource: The correct traffic sign to observe the right of way is number two. Number one indicates that a dangerous turn is in front of the driver while number three indicates the number of a national highway.
answerASource: Traffic sign 2
answerBSource: Traffic sign 1
answerCSource: Traffic sign 3

questionTextSource: What is allowed on priority roads outside built-up areas?
explanationSource: On priority roads outside of built-up areas, you may: 1. wait at the right side of the road; 2. park on a hard shoulder. On the other hand, parking on the road is prohibited. Parking vehicles constitute a hindrance and/or danger to flowing traffic, since vehicles are traveling much faster here than within the closed local area. Since a hard shoulder is not considered a lane, you may use it for parking. If there is a possibility to dodge on a hard shoulder, you should also use this for waiting shortly.
answerASource: Stopping on the right edge of the roadway
answerBSource: Parking on the hard shoulder
answerCSource: Parking on the roadway

questionTextSource: What ends here?
explanationSource: This traffic sign announces the change of a priority rule. It marks the end of the previous priority route. The signs "Give way" and "Stop! Give way!" that you can find on intersections and T-junctions, have a similar meaning.
answerASource: A priority road
answerBSource: A controlled parking zone
answerCSource: All previous zonal prohibitions

questionTextSource: What do you do when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: The traffic sign gives you priority over oncoming traffic. The oncoming traffic is obliged to wait in front of the obstacle. Nevertheless, you must prepare for stopping when oncoming traffic is already in the bottleneck. You have to let those drivers drive out. You may proceed with right of way if the bottleneck is free.
answerASource: You may only take precedence when the narrowing is clear
answerBSource: You must be ready to stop despite taking precedence
answerCSource: You must give precedence to the oncoming traffic

questionTextSource: What do these traffic signs allow?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates a parking lot that may only be used by residents with a correspondingly numbered parking pass. Other road users are not allowed to park there and have to switch to other parking places.
answerASource: Residents holding a parking licence with corresponding number may park here
answerBSource: All residents may use any of these parking spaces
answerCSource: Everybody may use this parking space as long as other parking spaces are still free

questionTextSource: Are you allowed to park here?
explanationSource: There are special parking spaces for people with impaired mobility or the blind. An official parking permit identifies the vehicles of these persons or their escorts. Even short parking on these areas is forbidden to other drivers.
answerASource: Yes, as a seriously disabled person with an official parking permit
answerBSource: Yes, as a person accompanying blind persons with an official parking permit
answerCSource: Yes, briefly for shopping

questionTextSource: What must you observe when parking here?
explanationSource: In this parking lot you are only allowed to park with a valid ticket. Attach the parking ticket legibly behind the front or rear window of your car. Be sure not to exceed the parking time stated on the ticket. The parking disc does not replace the parking ticket: the parking disc is only valid if the parking meter is out of operation.
answerASource: The parking time indicated on the parking ticket may not be exceeded
answerBSource: The parking ticket must be put in a place so it can be easily read when controlled
answerCSource: Parking ticket and parking disc rank equal

questionTextSource: Which vehicles are allowed to park here?
explanationSource: The traffic sign regulates the parking situation for the road user. The sign allows parking partly on the sidewalk, the side strip and on the green verge for vehicles up to a total weight of 2.8 tons.
answerASource: Vehicles with a permissible total mass of up to 2.8 t
answerBSource: Motorcycles
answerCSource: Vehicles with a permissible total mass of more than 3.5 t

questionTextSource: What must a motor vehicle's maximum speed, determined by its construction, be at least if you want to use this road?
explanationSource: You must be able to achieve at least a maximum speed of 60 km/h on a motor road with your vehicle. The beginning of a motor road is always marked by a traffic sign.
answerASource: 61 km/h
answerBSource: 51 km/h
answerCSource: 81 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This is a dead end. You can drive normally in a dead end, but the road ends so you might have trouble to turn your car around to leave the street again.
answerASource: A cul-de-sac
answerBSource: An underpass
answerCSource: A road closed to vehicle traffic

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign shows that a first aid station such as "Red Cross" or "Samaritan" is present at this point. However, it does not indicate any danger zone.
answerASource: A first aid station
answerBSource: A crossroads
answerCSource: A danger spot

questionTextSource: What must you remember when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: If you see this sign at lanterns, then you have to secure your vehicle with your parking light. Since these lanterns are not lit up all night, it's very unsafe to leave an unlit car here.
answerASource: Street lighting does not stay on all night
answerBSource: Vehicles may be parked here all night without lights
answerCSource: Vehicles may not be parked here

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign refers to a main road. The number stands for the number of the respective federal highway and not for example for a speed limit.
answerASource: A federal road (Bundesstraße)
answerBSource: Vehicles may not drive faster than 35 km/h
answerCSource: Vehicles must drive at least 35 km/h

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: The diversion arrow points out to you as a car driver that you are on a traffic jam-prone section of the motorway and points out an alternative route. But you are not obliged to follow this.
answerASource: An autobahn section which is normally not used very much
answerBSource: The place where the autobahn must be exited
answerCSource: An obligatory diversion for vehicles with dangerous goods

questionTextSource: During daylight, you enter a lit tunnel marked like this. What do you do?
explanationSource: As the car driver, you must turn on the low beam. The daytime lights are not enough, because the light conditions in the tunnel cannot be compared to daylight. You are obliged to make your vehicle seen.
answerASource: I switch on the dipped headlights
answerBSource: I do not have to switch on the lights
answerCSource: I switch on the side lights

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign shows you an emergency lay-by. There you can and should wait if you have a breakdown. It's possible that there's already a vehicle there. For breaks an emergency lay-by may not be used.
answerASource: There may be a vehicle in the next emergency bay
answerBSource: In case of breakdown, you should stop in the emergency bay indicated
answerCSource: You may stop here to have a break

questionTextSource: What do you have to do when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: This traffic sign is located in front of the entrance to a tunnel. Turn on the low beam and remove sunglasses for unrestricted visibility.
answerASource: Take off your sunglasses
answerBSource: Switch on dipped headlights
answerCSource: Switch on the hazard warning lights

questionTextSource: You drive into a brightly lit tunnel which is indicated like this. What do you do?
explanationSource: Even a brightly lit tunnel cannot reach daylight conditions, so you need to drive in with low beam to make your vehicle recognizable. The daytime running light is simply not enough in this case.
answerASource: Drive with dipped headlights
answerBSource: Drive without lights
answerCSource: Drive with daytime running lights

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination indicate?
explanationSource: The traffic sign points to the end of the traffic-calming zone. Now higher speed rules apply again. As a car driver, you have no right of way when you leave and must be careful when entering.
answerASource: - a traffic-calming zone
answerBSource: - a pedestrian zone
answerCSource: - a children's playground

questionTextSource: What does this traffic road sign mean?
explanationSource: You drive on a priority road. The traffic sign is repeated at each intersection or T-junction where you have priority. Outside built-up areas, parking on priority roads is prohibited. On these roads people drive relatively fast and parking vehicles would be a hindrance or a threat.
answerASource: Priority road
answerBSource: No parking on the carriageway outside of settlements
answerCSource: Expressway

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: This traffic sign indicates a numbered detour. Since there are several detours, they are numbered. So, you know exactly where to drive.
answerASource: A numbered diversion
answerBSource: Tram line no. 3
answerCSource: A numbered underground car park

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of when you see this traffic sign combination?
explanationSource: The priority road is turning due to the additional sign. It's a turning priority road. If you stay on the priority road, you will need to indicate that. You have to take care of pedestrians crossing the street, if necessary, you have to stop. The answer: "Driving to the left is mandatory" is wrong, since this traffic sign does not dictate a direction of travel.
answerASource: If I am following the priority road, I have to signal a left-turn
answerBSource: If I continue driving straight ahead, I have to signal a right-turn
answerCSource: The direction of traffic to the left is prescribed

questionTextSource: What is the correct action on roads with these types of protective cycle lanes?
explanationSource: The directional sign indicates the safe area for cyclists. As the vehicle driver, you may only drive on the designated bicycle lane if a risk to cyclists is ruled out. Parking is strictly prohibited here.
answerASource: - drive on the protective lanes when necessary, if there is no risk to cyclists
answerBSource: - in no event drive on the protective lanes
answerCSource: - use the protective lanes to assist parking

questionTextSource: What must you do upon seeing this traffic sign?
explanationSource: On pedestrian crossings pedestrians have precedence, so vehicles should always drive at moderate speed and be ready to brake at any time. There's a strict restriction on overtaking at pedestrian crossings and with halting traffic it is forbidden to come to a stop at the crossing.
answerASource: I must approach at a moderate speed if it is clear that pedestrians want to cross the road
answerBSource: I must allow the pedestrians to cross the road
answerCSource: I may not remain stationary on the pedestrian crossing if the traffic backs up

questionTextSource: What must you be prepared for here?
explanationSource: The traffic sign 'crossing guard', formerly school crossing guards, means that traffic assistance regulates traffic. They are allowed to stop traffic to allow children to cross the road. You need to be prepared for the flow of traffic to stop and for children to walk on the road more often than usual.
answerASource: That the flowing traffic will come to a stop
answerBSource: That children will cross the road more frequently than usual
answerCSource: That children will be playing on the road under supervision

questionTextSource: What applies according to this traffic sign?
explanationSource: Within traffic calming zones you are allowed to travel at a maximum walking speed of 4 to 7 km/h, which means first gear, without giving gas. The street may be used completely by pedestrians. Children are allowed to play in the street. Be especially attentive and ready to brake. Pedestrians must not be impeded or endangered, you may have to wait. Since playing on the street is allowed here, you have to pay more attention to children. You are only allowed to park on marked areas.
answerASource: I have to drive at walking speed
answerBSource: I may not impede pedestrians
answerCSource: I may only park on designated places

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign combination mean to you?
explanationSource: The traffic sign indicates the course of the priority road. The additional sign indicates the bend to the left; if you follow this direction, you will still be on the priority road.
answerASource: I am on a priority road that bends left
answerBSource: I am not permitted to turn right or drive straight ahead
answerCSource: If I continue driving straight ahead, I have to allow right of way to vehicles arriving from the right

questionTextSource: What do you have to be aware of when you see this traffic sign?
explanationSource: For this question, all three answers are correct. In a "traffic calming zone", pedestrians may use the entire width of the street, children can play on the street and you may only drive at walking speed.
answerASource: Children may play across the entire road
answerBSource: Motor vehicles have to drive at walking speed
answerCSource: Pedestrians are entitled to use the entire breadth of the road

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: When you see this traffic sign, you approach a customs office. This means that you may encounter a traffic jam and therefore have to reduce your speed. You may also be controlled, so have all the papers at hand. Follow the instructions of the customs officers.
answerASource: A customs point
answerBSource: A toll road
answerCSource: A prohibition on through-traffic

questionTextSource: What is correct in this situation?
explanationSource: The village sign indicates the end of a closed village in the lower part. The upper part indicates the next town. You are standing on the left side of the road.
answerASource: - a built-up area ends
answerBSource: - a built-up area starts
answerCSource: - a deviation ends

questionTextSource: What do you have to observe in a traffic situation like this one?
explanationSource: Guidelines have the task of separating the lanes. Therefore, you may only drive over them if you avoid obstacles or overtake vehicles. This is only allowed if no road user is endangered.
answerASource: - cross the centre line in order to pass by the obstruction
answerBSource: - cross the centre line in order to overtake
answerCSource: - not cross the centre line if this would endanger other traffic

questionTextSource: What does this traffic sign indicate?
explanationSource: If you are driving a vehicle, you may only stop in an emergency stop and breakdown bay in the event of an emergency or breakdown.
answerASource: An emergency stop and breakdown bay
answerBSource: A temporary additional carriageway
answerCSource: A stop for breaks and changing drivers

questionTextSource: You are driving on a road that has a protective lane for cyclists. How may you use the protective lane?
explanationSource: You may use the cycle lane if necessary, provided you do not endanger or obstruct cyclists.
answerASource: - drive on the protective lane when necessary
answerBSource: - park on the protective lane
answerCSource: - stop on the protective lane

questionTextSource: What can red-white warning plates on vehicles indicate?
explanationSource: Red and white warning signs point to vehicles with excess width (e.g., abnormal wide agricultural vehicles). Trailers parked inside a built-up area must either be illuminated or marked with reflective warning signs. So, warning signs do not refer to hazmat trucks.
answerASource: A trailer parked on the roadway within a built-up area
answerBSource: A vehicle with excessive width
answerCSource: A vehicle transporting dangerous goods

questionTextSource: What must you remember in the interests of environmental protection?
explanationSource: In the interests of environmental protection, you as a car driver must pay attention to the following: 1. Avoid driving unnecessarily; 2. Operate the engine evenly at medium speed; 3. Shut off the engine during extended periods of idling - after about a minute. In general, running the engine at idle will result in faster engine wear and wastes energy. You should therefore warm the engine in medium speed range and possibly stay in this area. High engine speeds lead to increased fuel consumption and therefore pollute the environment.
answerASource: To avoid running the engine unnecessarily when you are stationary
answerBSource: To avoid driving back and forth unnecessarily
answerCSource: To drive with high revolutions per minute too often

questionTextSource: What must be regularly serviced to prevent high fuel consumption and excessive pollutant emission?
explanationSource: The fuel consumption of a vehicle should be as low as possible. As a car driver, you should therefore regularly check and maintain all the components of your vehicle that are important for fuel consumption. These are, for example, the ignition and injection system and the engine air filter and the carburetor.
answerASource: Engine air filter
answerBSource: Carburettor or fuel-injection system
answerCSource: Ignition system

questionTextSource: What happens as a result of tyre pressures being well below the recommended levels?
explanationSource: Too low air pressure in the tire as well leads to increased fuel consumption. The effort required to keep the tire running is significantly higher than when the tire pressure has been set correctly. This leads to higher wear on the tire, increased fuel consumption and harms the environment.
answerASource: Fuel consumption increases
answerBSource: Tyre wear increases
answerCSource: Pollutant emission decreases

questionTextSource: When should you switch off the engine to save fuel and reduce pollution?
explanationSource: In order to avoid unnecessary consumption of gasoline, the engine should be switched off in certain situations. These include, for example, moments in traffic jams or in front of level crossings. Although there is an increased consumption during starting, it is compensated again starting from a downtime of one minute. You should not turn off the engine at a stop sign.
answerASource: When you have to wait in a traffic jam for a long time
answerBSource: When you have to wait at level crossings or building sites
answerCSource: When you stop at a "STOP" sign

questionTextSource: Your vehicle loses oil. How much drinking water can be polluted by a single drop of oil?
explanationSource: You should urgently avoid oil loss. Because even a single drop of oil can contaminate up to 600 liters of water and make it unfit for consumption.
answerASource: 600 litres
answerBSource: 50 litres
answerCSource: 1 litre

questionTextSource: What must you remember when refuelling?
explanationSource: Inhaling fuel vapors is harmful to your health. Therefore, make sure that the dispenser is being used correctly and pay attention to any defects when refueling. When the tank is full, the automatic system should be switched off. This prevents the overflow of gasoline or diesel. In addition, some air must remain in the tank for the fuel to expand.
answerASource: Do not breath in any fuel fumes
answerBSource: Do not refuel when the tank is full and the nozzle has switched off
answerCSource: Fill up to the brim if possible

questionTextSource: The engine and exhaust system of a vehicle have been "souped up". What could be the effects?
explanationSource: A souped-up engine and muffler provide increased noise pollution. In addition, the vehicle consumes more fuel. On top of that, exhaust emissions are increased and pollute the environment. In the worst case, the driver can expect a withdrawal of the operating license.
answerASource: Increased noise pollution
answerBSource: Increased pollutant emission
answerCSource: Considerable reduction in fuel consumption

questionTextSource: How can you reduce the noise made by a car?
explanationSource: In the low-speed speed range, the engine runs quieter than in high-revving areas. Deep-tread tires and driving without air filters also cause noise pollution. A driving style predominantly in the low-speed range saves fuel, is environmentally friendly and quiet. The minimum speeds must, however, be met, otherwise the engine runs twitchy, is cogging and sputtering. The lower the speed at which the engine is still running without cogging, the lower the noise.
answerASource: By driving at a low engine speed
answerBSource: By using all-terrain tyres
answerCSource: By removing the air filter

questionTextSource: Where can used oil, lubricants and car batteries be taken to be disposed of properly?
explanationSource: Waste oils, lubricants and vehicle batteries can be disposed of properly in the recycling center or via the car repair shop. Never should these substances be disposed of with household waste. It is special waste.
answerASource: To recycling centres
answerBSource: To car workshops
answerCSource: In the household waste

questionTextSource: How can you drive fuel-efficiently when travelling at a constant speed?
explanationSource: At constant speed, you can save fuel with the following measures: 1. by driving evenly in the low speed range; 2. by using the cruise control. The low speed range is just above the minimum speed that the engine needs for its optimal performance. If you drive below that, the engine is cogging; if you drive over it the fuel consumption increases in the high-speed range due to the engine performance. With steady driving in the low-speed speed range, the engine runs smoothly and with low fuel consumption. Cruise control allows a uniform driving style and relieves the / the car driver.
answerASource: By using cruise control
answerBSource: By driving at a low engine speed
answerCSource: By driving at a high engine speed

questionTextSource: At which engine speed range will fuel consumption be lower?
explanationSource: An engine consumes the least fuel in the speed range just above the minimum speed. This is the so-called low-speed speed range. When driving in the low-speed range, the speed should always be above the idle speed. An engine below the minimum speed runs twitchy, is cogging, sputtering or turns off. As a rule of thumb, the lower the speed at which the engine runs without cogging, the more fuel can be saved.
answerASource: At low engine speed
answerBSource: At high engine speed

questionTextSource: You have to wait because of a closed rail-crossing barrier. How can you now help protect the environment and save fuel?
explanationSource: When waiting in front of closed gates it is recommended to switch off the engine. On the one hand you save fuel and thus money, on the other hand, a switched-off engine is environmentally friendly. A pure gearshift to idle is not recommended, as even at idle the engine does not turn off and continues to use fuel.
answerASource: By switching off the engine
answerBSource: By switching off the automatic start-stop
answerCSource: By letting the engine idle

questionTextSource: What measures help protect the environment?
explanationSource: Turning the air conditioning off saves fuel as well. Low rolling resistance tires are designed for lower rolling resistance and can save fuel. Low-friction oils are synthetic oils with improved flow behavior, the so-called viscosity. This lubricates the engine faster and better and reduces friction. This also leads to a noticeable saving of fuel. Only oils approved by the engine/vehicle manufacturer should be used to prevent engine damage.
answerASource: - low-resistance tyres
answerBSource: - low viscosity engine oil
answerCSource: - air-conditioning systems

questionTextSource: What should you do to protect the environment?
explanationSource: You should avoid driving at high speeds. If the vehicle develops smoke, have it checked and repaired in a workshop.
answerASource: - a lot of smoke is coming from the exhaust, I should arrange for the motor vehicle to be checked
answerBSource: - waiting for prolonged periods, I should switch off the engine
answerCSource: - driving, I should use the upper engine speed range

questionTextSource: What can regular vehicle maintenance help achieve?
explanationSource: Regular vehicle maintenance helps to ensure road safety, low fuel consumption and low emissions.
answerASource: Keeping fuel consumption low
answerBSource: Keeping emission levels low
answerCSource: Ensuring road safety

questionTextSource: In which situations should you switch off the engine to save fuel and reduce environmental pollution?
explanationSource: You should turn off the engine before a gated railway crossing or if you are in a traffic jam, as you cannot accurately estimate the length of the waiting time. This way you can reduce your environmental impact and save fuel at the same time.
answerASource: - it is foreseeable that I will have to wait longer in a traffic jam
answerBSource: - I have to wait at a gated level crossing
answerCSource: - I have to stop at a “Stop” sign

questionTextSource: What can cause unnecessary noise pollution when driving?
explanationSource: If you drive at high speed or your muffler is defective, the noise level is increased. Low-speed driving also reduces fuel consumption. A defective muffler should be repaired in a workshop.
answerASource: Unnecessarily fast acceleration
answerBSource: A defective exhaust
answerCSource: Driving at low revolutions per minute

questionTextSource: How should you change gears in order to drive with particular consideration to the environment and fuel saving?
explanationSource: Changing gears environmentally friendly means to upshift early and shift down late. Do not drive the gears into the upper speeds, because this leads to the highest fuel consumption. You should start in first gear and then switch directly to second gear if possible. However, in some traffic situations, such as on the highway acceleration lane or while overtaking, it is necessary to drive with high speed. Of course you should do this in such a situation.
answerASource: Change to a higher gear as early as possible
answerBSource: Change to a lower gear as late as possible
answerCSource: Change gears only when reaching the upper revolution limit

questionTextSource: What is important to avoid pollution and to save energy when driving?
explanationSource: Low fuel consumption defines environmentally friendly and energy-saving driving. This requires the regular examination of consumption values ​​in order to optimize engine timing and to detect errors in good time.
answerASource: Check the fuel consumption regularly
answerBSource: Have the engine tuning checked regularly
answerCSource: Wash the engine regularly, at least four times a year

questionTextSource: How is fuel wasted and the environment polluted?
explanationSource: If you drive hectically, switching constantly between braking and accelerating and "playing with the gas", your vehicle consumes too much unnecessary fuel. Especially when accelerating a lot of energy is needed. When driving downhill in high gear, vehicle drivers burden the brakes unnecessarily. However, this does not result in increased fuel consumption.
answerASource: By driving in a hectic manner
answerBSource: By "playing with the accelerator" while waiting at traffic lights
answerCSource: Using a high gear driving downhill

questionTextSource: What indicates excessive petrol consumption due to high wear or wrong tuning of the engine?
explanationSource: Dark smoke indicates increased fuel consumption. It may be causally related to a fault of the engine control. You should definitely visit a workshop. Condensation, which drips from the exhaust, or white water vapor in cold weather, however, are normal.
answerASource: A lot of dark exhaust smoke
answerBSource: Condensation water from the exhaust

questionTextSource: What kind of driving results in high fuel consumption?
explanationSource: High speeds require more power due to the increased air resistance. As a result, the fuel consumption increases. If the driver drives rapidly or accelerates unnecessarily, this driving style also leads to an increased consumption. The same applies if gears are driven at full speed.
answerASource: Fast starts and unnecessary acceleration
answerBSource: Revving up all gears
answerCSource: Driving at high speed

questionTextSource: How do you drive with consideration to the environment?
explanationSource: Avoid constant acceleration and braking. Accelerating consumes unnecessary fuel and can be avoided, for example, in front of red lights by laying off the gas pedal. Maximum acceleration leads to high pollutant emissions and high noise as well as environmental pollution.
answerASource: Decelerate in good time and use the momentum of the car when the next traffic light is on red
answerBSource: Avoid unnecessary acceleration and unnecessary braking
answerCSource: Always accelerate to the maximum when the traffic light changes to green

questionTextSource: How can you cover more kilometres on less fuel?
explanationSource: The least fuel is consumed in the low-speed range. Timely upshifting avoids high-speed, fuel-intensive driving. Anticipatory driving saves fuel, as you can lay off the gas pedal early and let the vehicle roll out with its existing momentum.
answerASource: Accelerate in such a way that the highest possible gear is quickly reached
answerBSource: Think ahead when driving in order to avoid unnecessary acceleration and braking
answerCSource: By pushing all gears to the highest possible revolutions

questionTextSource: What can help save fuel and reduce pollution?
explanationSource: Before buying a car, the driver can check which model consumes little fuel. In addition, economical driving also contributes to savings.
answerASource: Paying attention to the fuel consumption of a motor vehicle when buying it
answerBSource: Contributing to a constant flow of traffic by thinking ahead and driving correspondingly
answerCSource: Using public transport, a bicycle or walking, wherever possible

questionTextSource: What can you do to help protect the environment?
explanationSource: The best way to protect the environment is to drive less. All journeys that are not absolutely necessary should be avoided. Certain driving styles consume extra fuel: Short-distance driving, especially when the engine is still cold, stop-and-go traffic, for example in crowded inner cities and searching for a parking space. On top of that, in areas with heavy traffic, the risk of smog from the exhaust emissions of vehicles increases. Driving at full throttle will not only cause noise, but your fuel consumption will increase disproportionately. However, once you are only 20% below the maximum speed, you reduce your fuel consumption by about 40%. Carpooling is also good for the environment.
answerASource: - driving short distances
answerBSource: - driving at flat-out speed
answerCSource: - driving in crowded city centres

questionTextSource: How can you achieve efficient fuel consumption and low pollutant emissions?
explanationSource: Before you drive off, you should make sure that the correct tire pressure is set. All important information about this topic can be found in the operating instructions of your vehicle. If the tire internal pressure is too high or too low, there will be an increase in pollutant emissions. Even unbalanced driving can lead to high fuel consumption. Therefore, instead of accelerating to the upper rotational speed limit and only change gears then, you should shift up in the lower or middle rotational speed limit range. This means you consume less fuel and at the same time reduce the exhaust emissions of your car.
answerASource: By having the proper tyre pressure
answerBSource: By maintaining an even driving style at low engine speeds
answerCSource: By using the total capacity of every gear

questionTextSource: Other road users draw your attention to the persistent thick smoke coming from your exhaust. What do you have to do?
explanationSource: You should visit a workshop that checks the damage. Because the strong smoke is a sign that the engine or the fuel system has a defect. In addition, the escaping gases are harmful to the environment and the smoke can obstruct the view of other road users.
answerASource: Have the vehicle promptly checked
answerBSource: Change down gears early
answerCSource: Change up gears early

questionTextSource: Why is it beneficial to use low viscosity engine oil?
explanationSource: Low-friction oils are characterized by a better flow behavior. These are synthetic oils that reduce friction in the engine through their improved viscosity and optimize oiling. This saves fuel. When using low-friction oils, attention must be paid to the approval of the manufacturer of the engine or vehicle.
answerASource: - generate less friction in the engine
answerBSource: - reduce fuel consumption
answerCSource: - can be disposed of as household waste

questionTextSource: What should you do after starting the vehicle?
explanationSource: After starting the car, you should drive off directly in the low-speed range. Warming up the engine while standing is prohibited and wastes fuel. In the low-speed range, the engine can be warmed up gently. Driving at high-revving directly is not good for the engine. Generally, the engine consumes more fuel at high speed. At too low speeds, the engine is cogging and sputtering. The consumption of an engine is the lowest just above the minimum speed.
answerASource: - drive off as promptly as possible
answerBSource: - drive at low engine speeds where possible
answerCSource: - if possible, allow the engine of the stationary vehicle to warm up

questionTextSource: How can you save fuel?
explanationSource: You can save fuel by: 1. driving smoothly and anticipatory while avoiding high engine speeds; 2. remove unused roof structures, as they increase fuel consumption with air resistance; 3. swap winter tires for summer tires in the warm season. Driving smoothly means avoiding unnecessary acceleration and deceleration. For example, by slowly rolling toward a red light, you can use the rest of the momentum to drive on if it is already green when you reach the traffic light. Only having to give gas, saves fuel compared to starting from standing completely still.
answerASource: - removing any non-essential roof boxes etc
answerBSource: - driving in a foresighted manner
answerCSource: - driving with winter tyres the whole year round

questionTextSource: How can you minimise tyre wear?
explanationSource: Driving with winter tires in non-winter weather leads to increased tire wear. Strong acceleration is associated with increased abrasion. The tire pressure recommended by the vehicle manufacturer must be observed. Too little tire pressure increases the tire wear considerably. If the air pressure is too low, the tire will deform more with each rotation than with correct tire pressure. Thereby the fabric layers of the tires rub against each other. Heat can be generated that irreparably damages the tire.
answerASource: I refrain as much as possible from rapid acceleration
answerBSource: I regularly check the tyre inflation pressure
answerCSource: I use winter tyres all year round

questionTextSource: What vehicles are generally exempt from the ban on road traffic in "environmental zones"?
explanationSource: A traffic ban for "environmental zones" does not apply to ambulances that are on duty. Also not affected are tractors from agriculture and forestry.
answerASource: Agricultural and forestry tractors
answerBSource: Ambulances on duty
answerCSource: All motor vehicles belonging to the environmental zone inhabitants

questionTextSource: When is it sensible to switch off a combustion engine?
explanationSource: Longer waiting times before a closed level crossing as well as loading and unloading a vehicle justify turning of an internal combustion engine. This saves fuel and reduces noise. The waiting time at a stop sign is usually short and you can usually drive on quickly after letting cross traffic pass. Therefore, in this situation, the internal-combustion engine is not turned off.
answerASource: During lengthy waits at rail crossings
answerBSource: When loading and unloading the vehicle
answerCSource: When stopping at intersections with a stop sign

questionTextSource: You are driving through city traffic in rush hour. What are the effects of stop-and-go driving?
explanationSource: The fuel consumption in stop-and-go traffic is greater, because drivers have to brake and accelerate more often. This leads to more exhaust fumes and other road users may be disturbed.
answerASource: - consumes more fuel
answerBSource: - creates more exhaust gases
answerCSource: - creates more nuisances for others

questionTextSource: What is a pre-condition for environmentally-friendly and energy-saving driving?
explanationSource: Tyre pressure has a major impact on fuel consumption as it affects rolling resistance. Regular vehicle maintenance contributes to low fuel consumption and emissions.
answerASource: Checking the tyre inflation pressure regularly
answerBSource: Performing vehicle maintenance regularly
answerCSource: Washing the engine regularly

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a manual gearbox. What style of driving results in higher fuel consumption within a built-up area?
explanationSource: When accelerating hard, you consume more fuel than when accelerating normally. The higher the speed, the more fuel is consumed.
answerASource: Accelerating heavily
answerBSource: Driving at high engine speed
answerCSource: Driving in a high gear

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a manual gearbox. How can you reduce the fuel consumption of your vehicle?
explanationSource: The higher the engine speed, the more fuel is consumed. If you ride with foresight, you can avoid unnecessary acceleration and braking.
answerASource: By switching early up to the next gear
answerBSource: By employing an anticipatory driving style, to avoid frequent changes of speed
answerCSource: By driving at a high engine speed

questionTextSource: How can you reduce the noise generated by your motor vehicle?
explanationSource: The higher the engine speed, the louder the engine will be. Tyres have differences in quality. When buying tyres, pay attention to the rolling noise.
answerASource: By driving at a low engine speed
answerBSource: By using tyres that have a low rolling noise
answerCSource: By removing the air filter insert

questionTextSource: What should you do to help protect the environment?
explanationSource: The engine also burns fuel when stationary, you should switch off the engine if you stop for a longer period of time. If there are atypical signs, such as smoke, you should have your vehicle checked.
answerASource: - have the motor vehicle inspected if the exhaust is emitting an excessive amount of smoke
answerBSource: - switch off the engine if waiting for longer periods
answerCSource: - avoid unnecessary driving back and forth

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a manual gearbox. When driving, what can cause avoidable noise pollution?
explanationSource: The higher the engine speed, the louder the engine. Tuned exhaust systems are usually louder than the standard exhaust system. You will consume more fuel when accelerating hard than when accelerating normally.
answerASource: Heavily accelerating unnecessarily
answerBSource: Defective silencer
answerCSource: Driving fast in low gears

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a manual gearbox on a level carriageway. How should you change gears so as to drive in a particularly environmentally conscious and economical way?
explanationSource: When accelerating, always try to keep the engine speed as low as possible. When coasting, shift gears as late as possible to take advantage of the engine braking effect and use the brakes as little as possible.
answerASource: Changing up gears as early as possible
answerBSource: Changing down again as late as possible
answerCSource: Drive until the upper engine speed range is reached in one gear, before changing up

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a manual gearbox. What type of behaviour pollutes the environment?
explanationSource: Constant speed consumes less fuel than constant deceleration and acceleration. Unnecessary acceleration at red lights burns fuel unnecessarily.
answerASource: An uneven driving style
answerBSource: Frequently pressing the accelerator while waiting at traffic lights
answerCSource: Driving downhill in a high gear

questionTextSource: You are driving a motor vehicle with a manual gearbox. How can you achieve efficient fuel consumption?
explanationSource: At constant speed, less is consumed than when constantly decelerating and accelerating. Tyre pressure has a major impact on fuel consumption as it affects rolling resistance.
answerASource: By having the proper tyre inflation pressure
answerBSource: By adopting an even driving style with low engine speeds
answerCSource: When accelerating, changing the gears upwards as late as possible

questionTextSource: What should you consider after starting your vehicle?
explanationSource: Unnecessary standing with the engine running unnecessarily pollutes the environment. Start the engine only when you want to drive off. The lower the engine speed, the less fuel is burnt.
answerASource: - drive off as promptly as possible
answerBSource: - drive at low engine speeds
answerCSource: - if possible, allow the engine of the stationary vehicle to warm up

questionTextSource: What influences the degree of centrifugal force on bends?
explanationSource: The centrifugal force can throw your vehicle out of the curve. When doubling the speed, the centrifugal force quadruples. In addition, the curve radius influences this force as well. The tighter the curve, the more the centrifugal force increases. Therefore, you must brake early in tight corners.
answerASource: The radius of the bend
answerBSource: Speed
answerCSource: Headwind

questionTextSource: Wet conditions can impair the effectiveness of drum brakes. What should you do?
explanationSource: The drum brake can run dry. For that you as the car driver brake cautiously. The drum brake heats up and the water can evaporate. Of course, you brake only when the traffic flow allows it. A rear-end collision must be avoided at all costs. Keep an eye on the traffic behind you.
answerASource: I carefully drive to dry the brakes
answerBSource: I apply the brakes repeatedly while stationary
answerCSource: I refill the brake fluid

questionTextSource: When will offences carrying two points be deleted from the Central Register of Road Traffic Offenders?
explanationSource: Registered misdemeanors, which have been scored and entered in Register of Driver Fitness with two points, become statute-barred after five years. Serious violations are always punished with two points.
answerASource: - 5 years
answerBSource: - 3 years
answerCSource: - 2 years

questionTextSource: What can cause skidding on bends?
explanationSource: Defective shock absorbers can be the cause of a vehicle skidding or swinging off the curve when turning. The correct tire pressure specified by the manufacturer is important for a stable vehicle position in curves. It is tailored to the overall design of the vehicle. Too low or too different tire pressure reduces driving stability and increases the risk of skidding on turns. Too high a load has an influence on the driving stability. There is not too little loading.
answerASource: Faulty shock-absorbers
answerBSource: Insufficient or uneven tyre pressure
answerCSource: Load is too small

questionTextSource: What can cause skidding on bends?
explanationSource: In curves, the following factors can lead to skidding: 1. excessive speed; 2. wet or icy roads; 3. strong braking. In turns, much of the stiction forces are needed for the cornering forces that hold the vehicle in the lane. If you brake too much, the stiction is instead used largely for the transmission of the braking force. As a result, the cornering forces are too low to counteract the centrifugal force, the same applies to excessive speed. On wet roads, a film of water between the road surface and the tire reduces grip and promotes skidding.
answerASource: Heavy braking
answerBSource: Driving fast on a wet roadway
answerCSource: Slight acceleration when leaving the bend

questionTextSource: What can cause a dangerous situation?
explanationSource: When cutting a corner, the vehicle in the curve is directed inward and the opposite lane is used as well in order to progress faster. This represents a serious threat to oncoming traffic and can lead to serious accidents. Dimming too late can also jeopardize oncoming drivers as they are dazzled and drive almost blindly.
answerASource: Cutting corners
answerBSource: Dipping your headlights too late
answerCSource: Driving too far behind the vehicle in front

questionTextSource: What do you do if the white car does not stop?
explanationSource: Even if you are in the right of way: Do not take risks in case of bad traffic behavior of other participants. Draw attention to the error with horn signal, but do not insist on your right and renounce your right of way. Lower your speed as well.
answerASource: Reduce speed, waive your priority
answerBSource: Sound your horn and try to pass in front of the car
